title: Pseudotrimezia pusilla
taxonomy: Pseudotrimezia pusilla is classified within the family Iridaceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudotrimezia, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The species P. pusilla is a small, perennial plant found in the tropical regions of South America. It is further classified as a member of the tribe Irideae, within the family Iridaceae. Pseudotrimezia pusilla is a relatively understudied species, and further research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.
